articles.title: Fat and Muscle Combo Can Be Dangerous
articles.summary: Scientists found that having too much belly fat and not enough muscle can make people more likely to die. This mix is called sarcopenic obesity. It happens when muscle gets smaller and fat gets bigger, especially in older adults. It can lead to problems like falling and feeling weak. The study looked at over 5,000 people for 12 years. They discovered that simple checks, like measuring waist size, can help find this condition early. This is important because it allows doctors to help with exercise and eating right to improve health. The research was done by universities in Brazil and the UK.

articles.title: Scientists Help Bees Grow Stronger with Special Food
articles.summary: Scientists from the University of Oxford and other places made a new food for honeybees to help them grow better. Bees need pollen from flowers to get important nutrients called sterols, but climate change and farming have made it harder for bees to find enough flowers. Beekeepers often give bees artificial food that doesn't have these sterols, so bees can become weak. The scientists used engineered yeast to create a special diet with the right sterols. When they tested it, bee colonies that ate this food produced up to 15 times more baby bees, called larvae, and stayed healthier. This new food could be a big help in saving bees from decline.

articles.title: A New Nail Polish for Using Phones with Long Nails
articles.summary: Scientists are making a special clear nail polish that lets people with long nails use touchscreens like phones and tablets easily. Usually, long nails don't work on screens because they are not conductive, meaning they can't carry electricity like fingertips do. This new polish has safe ingredients that make nails conductive so they can touch the screen. It was invented by a student named Manasi Desai and her teacher Joshua Lawrence at Centenary College. They wanted to solve a problem they saw when someone with long nails had trouble using a smartphone. The polish is clear, so it can go over any nail color or bare nails, helping people who have calluses too. They will share their findings at a big science meeting in 2026.

articles.title: A Mystery Under the Church Floor
articles.summary: In a church in the Netherlands, workers fixing a cracked floor found a skeleton. It might be the grave of a famous French soldier named d'Artagnan, who was a real person from history and also a character in a book called The Three Musketeers. D'Artagnan died long ago during a battle, and no one knew where he was buried. The skeleton was found with a musket ball and an old coin, which are clues that it could be him. Scientists are now testing the bones to see if they match d'Artagnan's family. This discovery is exciting because it could solve a 350-year-old mystery about where this legendary musketeer was laid to rest.

articles.title: Should We Stop Trail Hunting? A Big Decision in England and Wales
articles.summary: The government in England and Wales is asking people what they think about stopping trail hunting. Trail hunting is when dogs follow a scent on a rag instead of chasing real animals like foxes. It started after a law in 2004 to protect animals. Some people, like animal welfare groups, want to ban it because they think some hunters use it as a trick to still hunt foxes illegally. They say this can hurt wild animals and pets. Other people, like those in the countryside, say trail hunting is fun and brings communities together. They argue it helps local businesses and creates jobs, adding money to the rural economy. Now, everyone can share their opinions in a public consultation to help decide.

articles.title: A Winter Without Snow: Why It Matters for the West
articles.summary: In 2026, many parts of the Western United States had very little snow during winter. This is called a snow drought. Snow is important because it acts like a natural water storage, melting slowly in spring and summer to provide water for farms, cities, and nature. Without enough snow, there is less water available when it gets hot and dry. Ski resorts had to close early, and fire officials are worried about summer fires. Temperatures were warmer than usual, so rain fell instead of snow in some places. Water managers are telling people they might get less water than normal this year. This snow drought is a big problem for the West's water supply.
